page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8-lssd is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 30 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB per core. A key hardware inclusion is the bundled 137 GB local NVMe SSD, which provides high-speed local storage directly attached to the instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25% and single-threaded gzip compression achieving top-tier status in the top 10%. Text processing performance is also highly competitive, placing in the top 10% of tested instances. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25%, which is mirrored by a lower Passmark memory score. Multi-core performance, web serving throughput, and database operations consistently register in the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-8-lssd offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation and fast local scratch space without the overhead of network-attached storage. The presence of advanced vector and matrix extensions, such as AVX-512 and AMX, enables efficient execution of specialized mathematical and cryptographic operations. This server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, web serving, and lightweight database operations that benefit from local NVMe SSD caching.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8-lssd is a general-purpose server configured with 8 vCPUs, 30 GB of RAM, and a bundled 137 GB local NVMe SSD. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 instance features 4 physical cores with hyper-threading. Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, particularly in gzip compression and text processing, which both rank in the top 10%. Conversely, large-block uncached memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, placing in the bottom 25%. The server's dedicated CPU allocation and advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and AMX, make it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring fast local storage. It is highly suitable for software compilation, web serving, and database operations.
